name: worldmap
status: PREVIEW — not wired into render.py yet. Pick this theme via the hub before adoption.
description: Pokemon-style top-down world map. Quest clusters = fenced regions on a varied landscape (grass, water, mountains, forest, beach). Cities = cluster lead quests. Towns = member quests. Castle at center = long-term mission. Cream background, dark outlines, Fredoka font, bobbing trainer at current position.

rpg_vocab.castle: long-term project goal (central, with flag + crown)
rpg_vocab.region: cluster of related quests (fenced area with regional label)
rpg_vocab.city: cluster lead / main quest of the region (large red roof + chimney)
rpg_vocab.town: individual quest in the region (small roof + colored gable)
rpg_vocab.trainer: you, at the current most-active quest (bobbing pawn)

regions.strategy: tag-or-domain clustering, like solar; one region per cluster, region biome chosen by cluster theme

positions._comment: Regions are anchored at corners + center castle. Towns inside each region are auto-flowed.

evolution_from: themes/pokemon — same design tokens (Fredoka, ink #2a3a4a, gold #ffd24a, grass #8fc97a, sand #e8c878), but multi-region instead of linear path. Replaces the single-route layout that proved messy at 70 quests.
wiring_steps_when_adopted: 1. Copy mocks/ogas-real-worldmap.html structure into themes/worldmap/route.html.tmpl with Jinja-style placeholders
2. Add region-detection logic to render.py (group_quests_by_tag, assign biome by tag-rule)
3. Add theme registration to themes/_shared/README.md theme registry
4. Test render on OGAS first (preview at /ogas/route.html with theme=worldmap)
5. Either replace `pokemon` for OGAS or ship as new option alongside
6. Bump version to 1, remove status:PREVIEW field
